zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Knightley de Charleton於1394年3月30日之前出生在英國斯塔福爾格諾薩爾時，他的父親William Knightley19歲，母親Anne De Charlton24歲。他於1417年之前與Cecily Fraunceys結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1460年1月4日在英國史洛普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Knightley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Knightly
meanings: English (Middlesex and Cambridgeshire): variant of Knightley, a habitational name from Knightley in Gnosall (Staffordshire). The placename derives from Old English cniht ‘knight, young man, retainer’ (genitive plural cnihta) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. This is the usual spelling of the name in Ireland, where it has been establishes since the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Charleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Charlton
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): habitational name from North Charlton or Charlton near Bellingham (both Northumberland) or from any of the many other places so named (there are over ninety examples in England), from Old English Ceorlatūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) of the peasants’. Old English ceorl denoted originally a free peasant of the lowest rank, later (but probably already before the Norman conquest) a tenant in pure villeinage, a serf or bondsman.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Knightley de Charleton was born before 30 March 1394, in Gnosall, Staffordshire, England, his father, William Knightley, was 19 and his mother, Anne De Charlton, was 24. He married Cecily Fraunceys before 1417. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 4 January 1460, in Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Knightley de Charleton est né avant 30 mars 1394, à Gnosall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, William Knightley, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Anne De Charlton, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Cecily Fraunceys avant 1417. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 4 janvier 1460, à Shropshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Knightley de Charletonが1394年3月30日以前にイギリススタフォードシュアイアグノサルで生まれた時，父William Knightleyは19歳，母Anne De Charltonは24歳であった。1417年以前にCecily Fraunceysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1460年1月4日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Knightley de Charleton(이)가 1394년 3월 30일 이전 영국 스태퍼드실르 뇰사에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Knightley(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne De Charlton(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1417년 이전 Cecily Fraunceys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년 1월 4일 영국 슈럽실르에서 사망했다.
